page: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features two shared vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is provided by a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD, and the network configuration includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The server achieves top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng, alongside top-tier results in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, due to its shared CPU allocation and dual-core configuration, multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and overall PassMark CPU scores fall into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth for large blocks is average, while small-block read-only memory bandwidth is weak.

In terms of workload suitability, the server is configured for static web serving, where it demonstrates average throughput, and light text generation with small language models. It is less suited for database operations, Redis SET operations, or multi-threaded processing, which all score in the bottom 25%. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model and moderate memory density offer a budget-friendly option for entry-level workloads, though resource contention on shared cores represents a performance tradeoff.
description: The Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring two shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is configured with 8.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 120 GB NVMe SSD. Performance benchmarks reveal a significant divergence: single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the top tier, whereas multi-core CPU performance, PassMark scores, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. Large-block memory bandwidth and static web serving throughput remain average. This resource profile offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads that do not require sustained multi-core execution. It is best suited for static web serving, basic single-threaded applications, and lightweight development environments, while demanding database and multi-threaded tasks are limited by the shared CPU allocation.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a general-purpose virtual server featuring two shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 120 GB NVMe SSD, suitable for basic single-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-2c-8g-120s is a virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 120 GB NVMe SSD, built for entry-level workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining shared AMD EPYC processing with local NVMe storage for single-threaded and general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Two shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 120 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression
Weak multi-core and database performance due to shared CPU allocation
Best suited for static web serving and lightweight single-threaded tasks
